name: Mobility-Data Epidemic Modeling and Surveillance
description: Uses mobile phone call records, human mobility traces, and social network data to model, map, and forecast the spread of infectious diseases like malaria, COVID-19, and flooding-driven epidemics. Spans big-data prediction methods, agent-based simulation, and impact evaluation of alerts on population movement. Recurring tension around big-data traps and data access in low-resource settings.

name: AI-Driven Vaccination and Immunization Uptake
description: Optimizes vaccination interventions and immunization coverage in LMIC contexts using influence maximization, intervention optimizers (e.g., ADVISER), and recall-based coverage estimation. Combines algorithmic resource targeting with real-world deployment and impact evaluation, often foregrounding fairness across gender and marginalization axes.

name: Restless Bandits and Resource Allocation for Public Health Outreach
description: Applies restless multi-armed bandits, reinforcement learning, and index-based allocation to distribute scarce health-worker attention and resources across underserved communities, especially maternal and child health programs. Emphasizes fairness, non-Markovian real-world dynamics, and evaluation of treatment allocation. A recurring computational-fairness cluster tied to specific field deployments.

name: AI-Assisted Medical Imaging Diagnosis in Low-Resource Settings
description: Develops deep learning systems for screening and triaging diseases from images and signals—chest X-rays for pneumoconiosis/silicosis, head-and-neck and pediatric cancers, cough sounds for pulmonary disease. Focuses on diagnostic support where specialists are scarce, spanning model development and sociotechnical integration into care pathways.

name: Maternal, Child, and Reproductive Health Digital Systems
description: Designs and evaluates digital tools for maternal and child health—tele-triage for pregnant women, self-directed learning for maternity workers, birth declaration systems, contraceptive discontinuation analysis, and mother support groups. Spans CS methods, ethnographic design, and impact evaluation with strong gender and marginalization framing.

name: Climate-Health and Air Pollution Sensing
description: Maps and predicts environmental health hazards—air pollution sources, emission hotspots, wildfire smoke, kiln industry emissions—and their respiratory and physiological health impacts using satellite imagery, sensors, and ML. Sits at the climate-change/health interface with recurring exposure-mapping and epidemic-prediction patterns.

name: Clean Cooking and Household Energy Transitions
description: Studies adoption of cleaner cooking technologies and e-cooking to reduce health and climate harms, using behavior-change models (health belief model), service design, and field assessments in informal urban and rural settings. Combines ethnographic problem discovery, behavioral modeling, and impact evaluation with gender framing.

name: Human-Centered Global Health Device Design
description: Uses prototyping, usability studies, and stakeholder engagement to design frugal medical devices and diagnostics for underserved contexts—optical schistosomiasis diagnostics, infant warmers, front-end device design. Centers ethnographic and participatory methods for hardware in resource-constrained health systems.

name: Designing for Stigmatized and Marginalized Care Recipients
description: Human-centered and sociotechnical design work supporting caregivers and patients facing stigma, displacement, or chronic-disease burden—caregivers of children with disabilities, Rohingya refugees, adolescents managing Type 1 diabetes. Foregrounds solidarity, dignity, and marginalization in health-related ICT design.

name: Secure and Agile mHealth Deployment in the Field
description: Addresses operational realities of deploying mobile health systems—data security classification, adoption by displaced persons, and operationalizing agile principles for complex public-health programs. Spans engineering, adoption, and program-management challenges of scaling mHealth in low-resource settings.
